findings.effort: A column in stops.txt; your scheduling software likely has it.
findings.fix: Set wheelchair_boarding to 1 (accessible) or 2 (not accessible) for every stop. A field survey can start with the busiest stops.
findings.what: 281 of 296 stops don't say whether a wheelchair user can board there.
findings.why: Riders who use wheelchairs can't plan a trip when accessibility is marked 'unknown'; apps show no information at all.

findings.effort: Often one default setting in your export.
findings.fix: Set wheelchair_accessible on every trip (most small-agency fleets are 100% accessible, so this is often a single default).
findings.what: 84 of 508 trips don't say whether the vehicle is wheelchair accessible.
findings.why: Even with accessible stops, riders need to know the bus itself can take them.

completeness.name: completeness
completeness.summary: 5% of stops state wheelchair accessibility (5% marked accessible). Fare data is published.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'trip_distance_exceeds_shape_distance' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Trip distance exceeds shape distance (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'trip_distance_exceeds_shape_distance_below_threshold' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Trip distance exceeds shape distance below threshold (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: Usually a re-export from your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Schedule a feed re-export now so the new calendar is live well before the current one ends.
findings.what: This feed's service calendar runs out within the next 30 days.
findings.why: If a new feed isn't published before it ends, trip planners will drop your agency and riders will see no service.

findings.effort: A field at a time; not urgent.
findings.fix: Review the flagged fields and fill in the ones your riders would use.
findings.what: Some files are missing recommended (not required) fields.
findings.why: Recommended fields like agency_phone or stop descriptions make the feed more useful to riders and trip planners.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'unsorted_stop_times' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Unsorted stop times (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: A quick look at the flagged files.
findings.fix: Check the flagged column names for misspellings of standard GTFS fields; remove them if they are vendor extras.
findings.what: Some files contain columns that are not part of the GTFS spec.
findings.why: Harmless to riders, but apps ignore these columns and they can hide typos in real column names.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'platform_without_parent_station' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Platform without parent station (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

correctness.name: correctness
correctness.summary: The MobilityData validator flagged 7 kinds of issues: 22 error, 4 warning, and 511 informational instances in total.

findings.fix: Re-export with a validity window that reaches at least 60 days out.
findings.what: Service data runs out in 12 day(s).
findings.why: When the calendar runs out, trip planners stop showing this agency. Fixing it now is calmer than after riders notice.

freshness.name: freshness
freshness.summary: Service data runs out in 12 day(s). Publish an updated feed soon or riders will lose trip planning.

realtime.name: realtime
realtime.summary: This agency publishes realtime, but the feed needs an access key we don't have yet. Nothing here counts against the grade.

top_fixes.fix: Re-export with a validity window that reaches at least 60 days out.
top_fixes.what: Service data runs out in 12 day(s).
top_fixes.why: When the calendar runs out, trip planners stop showing this agency. Fixing it now is calmer than after riders notice.

top_fixes.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'trip_distance_exceeds_shape_distance' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
top_fixes.what: Trip distance exceeds shape distance (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
top_fixes.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

top_fixes.fix: Set wheelchair_boarding to 1 (accessible) or 2 (not accessible) for every stop. A field survey can start with the busiest stops.
top_fixes.what: 281 of 296 stops don't say whether a wheelchair user can board there.
top_fixes.why: Riders who use wheelchairs can't plan a trip when accessibility is marked 'unknown'; apps show no information at all.
